name: Stryker Mutator (JavaScript/TypeScript Mutation Testing)

performance.notes: Incremental mode and test runner plugins speed up repeated runs

agent_readiness.notes: Mutation testing for JS/TS. npx stryker run. stryker.config.json (testRunner: jest/mocha/karma/jasmine). HTML report with mutation score per file. Incremental mode (--incremental) for repeated runs. TypeScript support via ts-node. Also supports: C# (Stryker.NET), Scala, Python (alpha). Mutation score = killed/(killed+survived). Dashboard: https://dashboard.stryker-mutator.io. The standard mutation testing tool for JavaScript/TypeScript projects.

gotchas: Slow on large codebases without incremental mode
Equivalent mutants inflate surviving count artificially
Requires test runner plugin configuration per project
HTML report can be large for big codebases
best_when: Identifying weak test assertions in React/Node projects
CI mutation score tracking with Stryker Dashboard
Before releasing npm packages or critical application code
avoid_when: Projects with slow Jest suites (multiplied by mutations)
When 80% line coverage already satisfies requirements
summary: Stryker is the standard mutation testing tool for JavaScript/TypeScript. npx stryker run with jest/mocha/karma plugins. HTML reports with per-file mutation scores. Incremental mode for repeated CI runs. Stryker Dashboard for team tracking. Mutation score = killed/(killed+survived). Reveals test gaps beyond coverage. Also supports .NET and Scala.
